meta.title: SolarSmart - Intelligent Balcony Solar Solutions
meta.description: Rationally reduce electricity costs with intelligent balcony solar systems. We analyze your situation and recommend the optimal system setup.

hero_title:
Rationally
reduce electricity costs
with intelligent
balcony solar systems

subHero.heading: Why a balcony solar system with battery storage makes sense
paragraphs: Balcony solar systems are one of the most rational ways to cut electricity costs. But without battery storage, potential remains unused.
Solar power is only available when the sun shines. Household demand is highest in the morning and evening - exactly when solar production is low.
With battery storage, solar energy generated during the day is stored and used later. This means: higher self-consumption, fewer grid purchases, faster payback.

energyFlow.heading: How the system works
energyFlow.description: Understand how solar energy is generated, stored, and used intelligently.

day.title: Daytime
day.description: Solar power is generated, consumed directly, and excess energy is stored in the battery.
day.imageAlt: During the day, the solar system charges the battery and powers the home

night.title: Evening & Night
night.description: The battery powers the household when solar generation is not available.
night.imageAlt: In the evening and at night, the battery powers the home

cards.monitoringDescription: Keep your energy production in view

videoSection.heading: The system works all year.
paragraphs: Even in winter, modern solar panels continue to generate electricity. With battery storage, this energy is used with maximum efficiency.

When winter solar generation is lower, the battery can additionally operate in combination with a dynamic tariff. It automatically charges at night when electricity prices are low and is used during the day when tariffs are higher.
Intelligent energy management selects the most economical operating mode and optimally combines solar generation, battery storage, and electricity tariffs.

products.heading: Installable anywhere
products.description: Solar panels can be mounted in different locations - perfectly adapted to your space.

items.title: No permit required
items.description: Plug-in solar devices follow simplified rules. Only registration in the market data register is required - no prior grid operator approval.

items.title: Also for rental apartments
items.description: The system is compact, installation is non-invasive, and connection is via a standard socket. Ideal for tenants.

items.title: Portable when moving
items.description: The system remains your property. It can be easily dismantled and reinstalled at a new location.

items.title: 24/7 monitoring
items.description: Full control of your energy production and storage via app. Real-time monitoring and intelligent control.

calculator.heading: Calculate your savings potential
calculator.description: Estimate how quickly a balcony solar system pays off - based on your individual situation.

posts.title: Dynamic tariff + intelligent battery
posts.excerpt: The system continuously analyzes electricity prices, solar production forecasts, and household demand. When prices are low, the battery charges; when prices rise, stored energy is used.

posts.title: We help with tariff switching
posts.excerpt: No dynamic tariff yet? We support your switch to a dynamic electricity tariff to unlock the full potential of your system.

items.question: How much electricity can a balcony solar system produce?
items.answer: Output depends on several factors: location in Germany, balcony orientation, number of panels, and panel power. A typical 2-panel system (about 800W) can generate around 600-900 kWh per year in Germany, depending on orientation and shading.

items.question: Is a battery really necessary?
items.answer: A battery is not mandatory but highly recommended. Without storage, you can only use electricity at the moment it is produced - usually around midday when demand is low. With a battery, self-consumption rises significantly and payback accelerates.

items.question: Do I need to register the system?
items.answer: Yes, registration is required - but simplified. You must register your plug-in solar device in the market data register (MaStR). Prior approval by the grid operator is not required (status: Solarpaket I reform).

items.question: Can tenants install a balcony solar system?
items.answer: Yes. The system is compact, installation is non-invasive, and connection is via a standard socket. In rental apartments, inform your landlord, but approval is generally not required for plug-in operation.

items.question: How long is the payback period?
items.answer: Payback depends on your consumption, electricity prices, and system setup. Typically, a balcony solar system with battery pays back within 4-7 years. Without battery, it can take longer.

items.question: What happens when moving home?
items.answer: The system remains your property and is portable. It can be dismantled and reinstalled at your new place - whether balcony, garden, or wall.

sections.heading: Solar generation and storage
paragraphs: Solar panels generate electricity from sunlight. This energy is first used directly in the household.
Excess electricity is automatically stored in the battery system - for later.

sections.heading: Use stored energy
paragraphs: When solar generation is low or unavailable, stored battery energy powers the household.
This enables solar energy use around the clock - even when the sun is not shining.

privacy.intro: We process personal data exclusively in accordance with applicable law, in particular the GDPR, the German Federal Data Protection Act (BDSG), and the TTDSG. This privacy policy applies to the website https://speicher-balkonkraftwerk.de.

sections.title: 2. Hosting and Server Log Files
paragraphs: This website at https://speicher-balkonkraftwerk.de is currently provided via GitHub Pages. The hosting provider is GitHub Inc., 88 Colin P Kelly Jr St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA.

When you access the site, technically required connection data (for example IP address, date/time, requested URL, referrer, browser information) may be processed to ensure secure and stable operation.

Processing is based on Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in secure and stable website operation). GitHub privacy policy: https://docs.github.com/en/site-policy/privacy-policies/github-general-privacy-statement

sections.title: 3. Contact Form
paragraphs: When you contact us via the contact form, we process the data you provide (name, email address, message) in order to handle your request.
We use FormSubmit for technical form delivery. Submitted data is transmitted to FormSubmit as a technical processor.
The legal basis is Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (pre-contractual communication) and, where applicable, Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (efficient request handling).

sections.title: 4. Recipients and Third-Country Transfers
paragraphs: Your data is disclosed only to recipients that require it for the relevant purpose (for example hosting or form service providers).
A transfer to the USA cannot be excluded, in particular for hosting and technical form delivery.
Where data is transferred to the USA, we rely on appropriate safeguards under Art. 44 et seq. GDPR, in particular EU Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC).

sections.title: 5. Cookies and Similar Technologies
paragraphs: At present, we do not use analytics or marketing cookies.
Technically necessary UI state information (for example temporary display settings) may be stored in the browser where required for operation of the website.

sections.title: 6. Retention Periods and Deletion
paragraphs: We store personal data only as long as necessary for the relevant purpose or as required by statutory retention obligations.
Contact inquiries are generally stored for 6 to 12 months after completion of processing, unless longer retention is legally required.

sections.title: 7. Your Rights
paragraphs: Subject to legal requirements, you have the right of access (Art. 15 GDPR), rectification (Art. 16 GDPR), erasure (Art. 17 GDPR), restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR), data portability (Art. 20 GDPR), and objection (Art. 21 GDPR).
Where processing is based on consent, you can withdraw that consent at any time with effect for the future.
You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority (Art. 77 GDPR).

sections.title: 8. Obligation to Provide Data
paragraphs: Providing personal data is generally voluntary. However, without certain information (for example an email address in the contact form), we may be unable to process your request.
